layers.name: providers
layers.description: External AI/media provider clients — only api_routes and services may import

layers.name: infrastructure
layers.description: Database, auth, guardrails — services and api_routes may import; UI must not import admin

layers.name: api_routes
layers.description: Next.js API route handlers — invoked only via HTTP, never imported

layers.name: services
layers.description: Internal business logic — default layer for all lib/** files

forbiddenEdgeMessages.ui->providers: UI must not import providers directly. Call via API route.
forbiddenEdgeMessages.ui->api_routes: UI must not import API route modules. Use fetch() instead.
forbiddenEdgeMessages.services->ui: Services must not import UI. Circular dependency.
forbiddenEdgeMessages.providers->ui: Providers must not import UI components.
forbiddenEdgeMessages.providers->api_routes: Providers must not import API routes.
forbiddenEdgeMessages.infrastructure->api_routes: Infrastructure must not import API routes.
forbiddenEdgeMessages.api_routes->ui: API routes must not import UI components.

exceptions.description: Audited exceptions. Each entry requires id, reason, owner, createdAt, expiresAt. Use 'never' for permanent structural exceptions. No permanent exceptions for legacy systems — those get a deadline.

files.reason: Guard module — defines FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS as constants and throws on match. Never executes them.
Fatal guard stubs — callSyncLabs/pollSyncLabs throw [FATAL] immediately. No execution path to SyncLabs API.
DEPRECATED_MODELS array — fal-ai/sync-lipsync is a string in a comment-annotated array, never referenced by execution code.
Director Core system prompt — enumerates forbidden systems as negative constraints passed to LLM. Not executed.
This manifest is the source of truth. Defines forbidden patterns by necessity.
The scanner reads forbidden patterns from the manifest to scan against. Self-referential by design.
Migration docs reference legacy provider names in historical context.
SQL comment references synclabs as historical provider — not executable.
Migration comment references legacy provider names in historical context.
SQL comment explains historical reason for public bucket (legacy provider fetch requirement).
Documents SYNCLABS_API_KEY as a deprecated variable to remove from env.
UI page pending migration to Director Core avatar flow. Backend API /api/cinematic/lipsync returns 410 — no execution path.
Creator dashboard pending migration. generateLipsync calls /api/lipsync which returns 410 — no execution path.
Standalone lipsync page pending removal. Calls /api/lipsync which returns 410 — no execution path.

generateLipsyncWithFallback is the new Hedra-based lipsync provider (Hedra → static fallback). Matches the banned 'generateLipSync' pattern because the forbidden pattern targeted SyncLabs. This function has zero SyncLabs execution path.
